titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in France ( (Leahy) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in France ( Leahy ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 451. For the Acting Secretary. The official mentioned in our 690, June 14, 10 a.m., 1941Not printed. in conversation with a member of this Embassy yesterday stated that since a previous conversation (see our 417, March 19, 5 p.m.Not printed.) German pressure on Vichy has become much more intense.

This pressure while not yet in ultimatum form is being applied through Benoist-Méchin,Jacques Benoist-Méchin, French Secretary of State to the President of the Council of Ministers. AbetzOtto Abetz, German Ambassador on special mission in Paris. and de BrinonFernand de Brinon, General Delegate of French Government for Occupied Territories. and is directed toward the establishment of a definitely collaborationist government prior to the spring offensive in Russia.

He said that Germany is pressing for the return of LavalPierre Laval, named Chief of French Government April 18, 1942. which is extremely displeasing to the MarshalMarshal Pétain, French Chief of State. who is, however, very friendly with René de Chambrun.Son-in-law of Pierre Laval.

Our informant fears that a definitely pro-collaborationist government will in the near future be forced upon the Marshal. He said also
that Xavier Vallat, Commissioner for Jewish Affairs, and Xucheu [Pucheu?]Pierre Pucheu, French Minister of Interior. have now been refused permission to travel between the occupied and unoccupied zones and that a decree putting an early end to the Riom trial will shortly be issued following German pressure.

The individual mentioned in our 1490, December 3, 2 p.m., 1941Not printed; the individual is identified only as a career diplomat and member of Marshal Pétain’s “entourage” (851.00/2529). told us today that Germany will no longer negotiate with the present Government, that a change will be made at a time chosen by Germany and that Laval will be returned to the Cabinet, the matter having already been discussed with the Marshal by René de Chambrun.

We are told by a reliable informant who obtained his information from Laval that the Marshal and Laval had a secret meeting this morning in the country near Vichy in which the question of Laval’s return to the Cabinet was discussed.
Arrangements have been made for Darlan to confer with Laval Saturday at Châteldon.
